contact.title: Contact Us
contact.intro: Have a question, spotted a bug, or want to suggest something? Email us at info@datorbuve.lv.

about.title: About DatorBuve
about.intro: DatorBuve is a free tool for browsing PC components, checking compatibility, and building a PC using real, current prices from Latvian retailers. It replaces scattered spreadsheets and forum threads with one place to plan a build with confidence.

sections.heading: Our Mission
sections.body: Building a PC means juggling compatibility (does this cooler fit this case? will this PSU handle this GPU?) and comparing prices across stores that all list things differently. DatorBuve exists to make that easier: one place to browse parts, get automatic compatibility checks, and see live pricing from Latvian stores.

sections.heading: What We Offer
list: Live pricing and stock status from Latvian retailers.
Automatic compatibility checks between the components you pick.
An auto-builder that assembles a complete, compatible build within your budget.
The ability to save your builds and share them with a link.

sections.heading: Who It's For
sections.body: First-time builders who aren't sure where to start, experienced builders who just want to compare prices and specs faster, and anyone building a PC for the Latvian market.

sections.heading: Who's Behind This
sections.body: DatorBuve is an independent project, not affiliated with or sponsored by any retailer or manufacturer. Component recommendations and compatibility checks are based purely on specifications, not paid placement.

sections.heading: Meet the Developer
sections.body:
Hi! DatorBuve is a small personal project, built in my spare time. The whole site, the compatibility checking system, the auto-builder, the price gathering, and everything else: all of it is built by one person.
The idea was simple: build a tool that makes it easier to put together a PC for the Latvian market.
If you have any suggestions, spot a bug, or have an idea worth adding, I'd love to hear about it. It'll help keep improving DatorBuve and make it more useful for everyone.

sections.heading: About Our Data
sections.body: Prices, stock, and specifications are collected from public listings at Latvian retailers and refreshed regularly, but they can lag behind what's actually on the retailer's site. Always double-check price and availability before completing a purchase. See our Terms of Service for details.

sections.heading: Feedback
sections.body: Found a bug, a wrong spec, or have a suggestion? We'd like to hear about it.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: DatorBuve ("we", "us") operates datorbuve.lv, a tool for browsing PC components, checking compatibility, and building and saving PC configurations. This page explains what personal data we collect, why, and what rights you have over it under the GDPR.

sections.heading: Data We Collect
list: Account data: name, email address, and a hashed password when you register. We never store your password in plain text.

Build data: the components, name, and optional notes of any build you save, and whether it's shared publicly. If you mark a build as public, its name, selected components, and notes become visible to anyone with the share link. Your account details (name, email) are never included or shown.
Cookies: see the Cookies section below.

sections.heading: How We Use Your Data
list: To create and secure your account, and to keep you signed in. Necessary for performance of a contract with you (GDPR Art. 6(1)(b)).
To save, display, and let you manage your PC builds. Necessary for performance of a contract with you (GDPR Art. 6(1)(b)).
To send account-related emails: email verification and account-deletion confirmation. Necessary for performance of a contract with you (GDPR Art. 6(1)(b)).
To protect sign-in and sign-up forms from automated abuse (Cloudflare Turnstile). Based on our legitimate interest in keeping the service secure (GDPR Art. 6(1)(f)).
To understand how the site is used, only if you've consented to analytics cookies. Based on your consent (GDPR Art. 6(1)(a)), which you can withdraw at any time.

sections.heading: Cookies
sections.body: We use a small session cookie to keep you signed in. This is strictly necessary and doesn't require consent. With your permission, we also use Google Analytics cookies (named _ga and _ga_...) to understand how the site is used. These are only set after you accept analytics cookies — nothing is loaded or stored before that. You can accept, reject, or customize this choice at any time using the "Cookie Settings" link in the footer; rejecting analytics after accepting also removes these cookies.

sections.heading: Third-Party Services
list: Cloudflare Turnstile: protects our sign-in and sign-up forms from bots. It processes your IP address and device signals for security purposes; see Cloudflare's privacy policy (cloudflare.com/privacypolicy) for details. This is used regardless of cookie preferences, as it's necessary for account security.

Google Analytics (Google Ireland Ltd.): measures aggregated site usage — pages visited and actions like generating or saving a build — so we can improve the site. It runs only if you consent to analytics cookies. See Google's privacy policy (policies.google.com/privacy) for details.

Resend: delivers our verification and account-related emails. It processes your email address and the email content for delivery purposes; see Resend's privacy policy (resend.com/legal/privacy-policy) for details.

sections.heading: International Data Transfers
sections.body: Some of our service providers, including Cloudflare, Google, and Resend, may process data outside the EU/EEA. Where this happens, we rely on appropriate safeguards required by the GDPR, such as Standard Contractual Clauses or the EU–US Data Privacy Framework, to ensure your data remains protected.

sections.heading: Data Retention
sections.body: We keep your account and build data for as long as your account exists. You can delete your account at any time from Account Settings. This permanently removes your account and saved builds after you confirm via an emailed link.

sections.heading: Your Rights
sections.body: Under the GDPR, you have the right to:
list: Access the personal data we hold about you.
Correct inaccurate data (via Account Settings).
Erase your account and data at any time (via Account Settings, self-service, no need to contact us).
Request a copy of your data in a portable format.
Object to or restrict certain processing.
Lodge a complaint with your local data protection authority: in Latvia, the Data State Inspectorate (dvi.gov.lv).

sections.heading: Data Security
sections.body: Passwords are hashed, not stored in plain text, and access to the database is restricted. No method of transmission or storage is 100% secure, but we take reasonable steps to protect your data.

sections.heading: Changes to This Policy
sections.body: We may update this policy from time to time. Minor wording fixes will simply update the date at the top of this page. For meaningful or substantive changes (such as new data uses or new third-party services), we'll notify you directly, by email, in addition to updating this page.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.body: Questions about this policy or your data?

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These terms govern your use of DatorBuve ("we", "us"), a tool for browsing PC components, checking compatibility, and building and saving PC configurations for the Latvian market. By using the site, you agree to these terms.

sections.heading: The Service
sections.body: DatorBuve lets you browse PC components, check compatibility between them, generate builds automatically within a budget, and save or share configurations. We are not a retailer. We don't sell components, process payments, or fulfill orders. Purchases happen on third-party store websites we link to.

sections.heading: Eligibility
sections.body: You must be at least 16 years old to create an account and use the service.

sections.heading: Accounts
list: You're responsible for keeping your password confidential and for all activity under your account.
You agree to provide accurate information when registering.
You may delete your account at any time from Account Settings. This permanently removes your account and saved builds.

sections.heading: Pricing & Availability Disclaimer
sections.body: Prices and stock information are collected from third-party retailer websites and may be outdated, inaccurate, or no longer available by the time you view them. Always verify the current price and availability on the retailer's own site before purchasing. We are not responsible for pricing errors or discrepancies.

sections.heading: Compatibility Disclaimer
sections.body: Compatibility checks, warnings, and auto-generated builds are provided as a best-effort convenience based on the specifications we have on file, and are not a guarantee of fit or function. Some checks are skipped when a component's specs are unknown (flagged as "needs manual check"). Always verify critical specifications yourself (dimensions, power requirements, socket type) before purchasing.

sections.heading: Saved & Shared Builds
sections.body: Build names, notes, and component selections are content you create. If you mark a build as public, that name, its components, and notes become visible to anyone with the share link. Don't include unlawful, offensive, or personal information in build names or notes.

sections.heading: Acceptable Use
list: Don't scrape, automate, or send excessive automated requests against the site.
Don't attempt to bypass security measures, including bot-protection on sign-in and sign-up.
Don't use the service for any unlawful purpose.

sections.heading: Third-Party Links & Affiliate Disclosure
sections.body: Links to retailer stores are provided for your convenience. We don't control, and aren't responsible for, the content, pricing, or transactions on third-party sites. Some of these links may be affiliate links. If you make a purchase through them, we may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.

sections.heading: Intellectual Property
sections.body: The DatorBuve name, design, and code are ours. Component names, images, and specifications belong to their respective manufacturers and retailers, and are used to help you compare products.

sections.heading: Limitation of Liability
sections.body: The service is provided "as is", without warranties of any kind. We are not liable for purchasing decisions made based on information from the site, for errors in third-party pricing or compatibility data, or for issues arising from third-party retailer websites. To the fullest extent permitted by law, we are not liable for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from your use of the service.

sections.heading: Account Termination & Service Changes
sections.body: We may suspend or terminate accounts that violate these terms. You may stop using the service and delete your account at any time. We also reserve the right to discontinue, modify, or remove any part of the service at any time, including without prior notice.

sections.heading: Severability
sections.body: If any provision of these terms is found to be unenforceable, the remaining provisions will remain in full effect.

sections.heading: Governing Law & Entire Agreement
sections.body: These terms are governed by the laws of Latvia. Together with our Privacy Policy, these terms make up the entire agreement between you and us regarding the service.

sections.heading: Changes to These Terms
sections.body: We may update these terms from time to time. Minor wording fixes will simply update the date at the top of this page. For meaningful or substantive changes, we'll notify you directly, by email, in addition to updating this page.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.body: Questions about these terms?
